title: Electric-Field-Driven Deformation Dynamics in Metal Nanostructures
objective: Understand and model how electric fields induce and control plastic deformation, lattice reconfiguration, and phase transitions in metallic nanostructures at sub-10nm scales.

papergraph.title: Spin dynamics in semiconductor nanostructures
papergraph.citation: M. W. Wu et al. (2006) Spin dynamics in semiconductor nanostructures arXiv:0611649v1
papergraph.abstract: We review our theoretical investigation on the spin relaxation/dephasing in spin precession and spin diffusion/transport in semiconductor nanostructures based on the kinetic spin Bloch equation approach.
papergraph.conclusion: We review our theoretical investigation on the spin relaxation/dephasing in spin precession and spin diffusion/transport in semiconductor nanostructures based on the kinetic spin Bloch equation approach.
papergraph.scenario: spin relaxation/dephasing in spin precession and spin diffusion/transport in semiconductor nanostructures
papergraph.contribution: We review our theoretical investigation on the spin relaxation/dephasing in spin precession and spin diffusion/transport in semiconductor nanostructures based on the kinetic spin Bloch equation approach.
credibility_reasons: credibility multiplied down by low publication quality
